meta.description: Curated AML typology cards. These are the retrieval unit for triage + verifier. Hand-authored, tiny, deterministic. NOT generated from PDF RAG.
meta.ctrThresholdNote: VERIFIED: BNM Cash Threshold Report, RM25,000, effective 1 Jan 2019 (reduced from RM50,000). Covers CASH only (excludes electronic transfers, cheques, bank drafts) and aggregates within the same day.
meta.note: Source strings below are document-level; tighten FATF/BNM section numbers against the source PDFs if time allows.

typologies.name: Pass-through / Rapid Movement
typologies.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.10/R.20; BNM AML/CFT Policy Document
typologies.definition: Funds are received and moved on almost immediately, leaving little or no balance retained. The account is a conduit, not a destination.
indicators: Inbound credit followed by outbound debit of a similar amount within a short window (minutes to a few days)
runningBalance spikes on the inbound then drains back to ~0
Little to no funds retained; no spending consistent with the stated purpose of the account
Often a newly opened or recently dormant account
dataSignals: Transaction.direction alternates inbound -> outbound with matching amounts
Transaction.runningBalance returns to near-zero after each cycle
Short delta between inbound timestamp and onward outbound timestamp
typologies.benignLookalike: High-turnover business sweeping daily takings to an operating/settlement account on a regular schedule.
typologies.distinguishingTest: Benign sweeps are REGULAR (same time, same destination, commercial rationale). Pass-through laundering shows IRREGULAR timing, DIVERSE counterparties, and no commercial rationale for the account holder profile.
strNarrativeHints: State the in/out amounts and dates

typologies.name: Fan-in / Fan-out (Consolidation)
typologies.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.20; BNM AML/CFT Policy Document; scam-mule guidance
typologies.definition: Many inbound transfers from unrelated parties are consolidated in one account, then moved out in fewer, larger transfers. Classic scam-victim consolidation / money-mule pattern.
indicators: Multiple inbound credits from many unrelated individuals over a short period
Funds aggregated then forwarded onward in one or few large outbound transfers
Senders have no apparent relationship to the account holder
Amounts and frequency inconsistent with the account holder's profile
dataSignals: High count of distinct Transaction.counterpartyName / counterpartyAccount on inbound legs
Inbound many-to-one followed by outbound one-to-few
runningBalance climbs through inbound legs then drops sharply on outbound
typologies.benignLookalike: Legitimate merchant or crowdfunding account receiving many small customer payments.
typologies.distinguishingTest: Merchants receive payments FOR goods/services (consistent amounts, business account type, retained/working balance). Mule consolidation shows unrelated PERSONAL senders, rapid full forwarding, and a personal account type.
strNarrativeHints: Count and characterise the inbound senders

typologies.name: Structuring / Smurfing
typologies.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.20; BNM AMLA 2001; CTR threshold RM25,000
typologies.definition: Transactions are deliberately broken into amounts just below a reporting threshold to avoid triggering a Cash Threshold Report (CTR).
indicators: Multiple CASH deposits clustered just under the RM25,000 CTR threshold
Repeated similar sub-threshold amounts over a short period
Spread across DAYS, BRANCHES, or multiple PERSONS (smurfing) to defeat same-day aggregation
Total aggregated value is substantial
dataSignals: Transaction.channel == cash / branch deposits (CTR is cash-only; transfers do not trigger it)
Cluster of similar amounts spread across separate days (same-day cash is auto-aggregated by BNM)
typologies.benignLookalike: A business making genuine recurring sub-threshold payments (e.g. supplier instalments) or salary/rent flows.
typologies.distinguishingTest: Benign recurring payments have CONSISTENT counterparties and a documented commercial reason. Structuring shows CASH amounts SUSPICIOUSLY clustered just below RM25,000, deliberately spread across days/persons, with no reason other than avoiding the report.
strNarrativeHints: List the sub-threshold amounts and dates
State the RM25,000 threshold being avoided

typologies.name: Dormant-then-Active
typologies.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.10 (ongoing CDD); BNM AML/CFT Policy Document
typologies.definition: An account inactive for a long period suddenly shows high-value or high-volume activity, often without any update to customer due diligence.
indicators: Long period of no/low activity followed by sudden large throughput
Reactivation not accompanied by a KYC/CDD refresh
Funds often move straight out after arriving
Activity profile inconsistent with the account's history
dataSignals: Large gap between Account.openedAt / last activity and the alert transactions
Sudden jump in Transaction.amount and frequency
Inbound immediately followed by outbound (overlaps PT-01)
typologies.benignLookalike: A seasonal business, or a genuine one-off large legitimate transaction (property purchase, inheritance) with supporting documentation.
typologies.distinguishingTest: Benign reactivation has a DOCUMENTED rationale and often retains funds or spends them on the stated purpose. Suspicious reactivation has NO rationale, no CDD update, and rapid onward movement.

typologies.name: KYC Profile Mismatch
typologies.source: FATF Recommendations (2012), R.10 (CDD); BNM AML/CFT Policy Document
typologies.definition: Transaction volume, value, or type is materially inconsistent with the customer's stated occupation, income, or declared account purpose.
indicators: Throughput far exceeds declared income or expected activity
Transaction types inconsistent with account type (e.g. heavy international wires on a basic personal account)
Counterparties or geographies inconsistent with the customer profile
Stated occupation does not explain the flows
dataSignals: Aggregate Transaction.amount over period >> plausible income for Account.holderName profile
Account.accountType inconsistent with channel / counterparty mix
typologies.benignLookalike: A customer whose genuine circumstances changed (new job, business growth, inheritance) and who can document it.
typologies.distinguishingTest: A genuine profile change is EXPLAINABLE and documentable. A mismatch typology is flagged when the activity has NO consistent explanation against the known profile and no updated CDD on file.
strNarrativeHints: State the declared profile vs observed activity
